Nadhiyil vizhum ilai indha kadhalaa

vilundhadhu naanaa?

ezhundhidum podhum vilundhiduvenaa!!

kidaithiduvenaa ?

pengal manam oru oonjal illai
oonjal thannaal asaivadhillai
ilupadhu neeyaa ?
varuvadhu naanaa ?
dhisai ariyaadhu thirumbiduvenaa ?
kadhalin oonjalil asaivadhu sugam sugam .

enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the enchanting melody of Aaha Kaadhal from the movie Moondru Per Moondru Kadhal. This soulful track, composed by Yuvan Shankar Raja and penned by Na Muthukumar, captures the essence of love and longing. Let the soothing voice of Nandini Erikar transport you to a world of romance and serenity.
stanzaMeanings: Ah, love, softly whispers, expressing its emotions.

Love, my love, I live for you. Ah, love, it gently showers like rain, making the heart flutter. Stop the song lyrics in Tanglish. Exact translation of Tamil words: Ah, love, softly speaks, teasingly showing its mischievous nature. A single name is uttered by the lips, and my name merges with it. In the depths of questions, victory dances. Is this the question love asks?
Ah, love, softly speaks, teasingly showing its mischievous nature.
A leaf falls into the river; is this love? Is it the first time it touches the shore? Was it me who fell? Will I rise again? Even if I rise, will I fall again?
I don't know if I see you, but I see you differently. Are you with me? Am I with you? Am I you? Are you me? What is this joy? Every day brings happiness.
Ah, love, softly speaks, teasingly showing its mischievous nature.
Something drew me to you, and that brought you to me. Was it me who surrendered? Will I achieve it? Even if I achieve it, will I surrender again?

The heart of women is like a swing; it doesn't move without a push. Are you the one to push it? Am I the one to come? Without knowing the direction, will I turn back? Swinging in the cradle of love brings happiness.

Ah, love, softly speaks, teasingly showing its mischievous nature. A single name is uttered by the lips, and my name merges with it. In the depths of questions, victory dances. Is this the question love asks?
Ah, love, teasingly showing its mischievous nature.
